handshape: 1-hand — index finger extended pointing
location: Neutral space to the side of body
movement: Repeated lateral wagging right to left
facial_expression: Eyebrows raised throughout entire hand movement
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — eyebrow raise is grammatical WH-marker
annotator_notes: Eyebrow raise sustained across full sign duration confirming grammatical scope. Mouthing of spoken word co-occurs — unclear whether obligatory or signer-specific contact phenomenon with English. Video angle makes it difficult to distinguish eyebrow raise from wide-eye opening precisely.

handshape: Three fingers extended loosely hanging — not cleanly folded; thumb position unclear from video angle
location: Mouth — hand makes contact or near-contact
movement: Repeated forward and back movement toward mouth
mouth_pattern: O-shape synchronised with hand at mouth position
non_manual_obligatory: Unclear — O-shape may be co-articulatory effect of hand approaching mouth
annotator_notes: Handshape differs from citation W-hand — fingers hang loosely rather than cleanly tucked. Thumb not visible clearly from video angle. Mouth O-shape appears specifically at moment of hand-mouth proximity not throughout — raises question of phonological mouth pattern vs co-articulatory effect. Single camera angle limits confidence.

handshape: Both S-hands closed fists — knuckles of dominant right hand face forward
location: Neutral space — right knuckles contact back of left hand
movement: Dominant hand hits back of non-dominant hand three times repeated tapping
mouth_pattern: Signer mouths 'work' — three mouth movements synchronised with hits
head_movement: Head nod three times synchronised with each hit
non_manual_obligatory: Unclear — head nod and mouthing may be prosodic/emphasis rather than grammatically obligatory
annotator_notes: Head nod synchronised beat-for-beat with hand movement — three nods three hits. Raises question of whether head movement is prosodic beat marker rather than lexical/grammatical NMM. Mouthing pattern consistent across WHERE WATER WORK — suggests signer-specific habit or English contact influence.

handshape: Open-5 hand both — all fingers extended spread
movement: Static bilateral hold both hands contact chest
non_manual_obligatory: Possible — body contact location may be phonologically contrastive
annotator_notes: Kinship signs in GSL frequently use body-contact location as a phonological feature. Mouthing of 'mum' consistent with contact phenomenon observed across dataset.

handshape: Open-5 hand — all fingers extended
movement: Static hold or single tap at forehead
non_manual_obligatory: Possible
annotator_notes: Location at forehead contrasts with MOTHER at chest — minimal pair candidate on location parameter. Annotated from author's own GSL knowledge cross-referenced against GNAD dictionary.

handshape: Flat-O / bunched hand — fingertips meet thumb
movement: Repeated path toward mouth depicting eating action
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — mouth pattern is semantically integral to sign
annotator_notes: Mouth pattern for EAT is widely documented as obligatory across sign languages with ASL influence. Iconic: hand shape and movement depict food entering mouth.

handshape: Open-B both hands — flat fingers together
movement: Hands converge toward face head tilts laterally
facial_expression: Eyes closed or lowered — semantically obligatory
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — eyes closed is semantically obligatory
annotator_notes: Facial expression (eyes closed) is semantically obligatory — signing SLEEP with open eyes would be marked or ungrammatical. Head tilt co-occurs consistently in documented productions.

handshape: Open-B / 5-hand both — fingers extended spread
movement: Upward circular movement on chest
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — smile is semantically contrastive with SAD
annotator_notes: Facial expression semantically obligatory and contrastive with SAD which uses downturned mouth. Annotated from author's own GSL knowledge.

handshape: Open-5 hand both — all fingers extended spread
movement: Downward bilateral movement along cheeks
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — facial expression semantically contrastive with HAPPY
annotator_notes: Downward movement of hands mirrors downward facial expression — strong iconic mapping between manual and non-manual features. Contrastive pair with HAPPY.

handshape: Open-B both hands — flat fingers together
movement: Repeated clap-like movement
non_manual_obligatory: No — no obligatory NMM documented
annotator_notes: Low iconicity sign. No obligatory non-manual marker. Arbitrary form consistent with institutional/cultural signs that lack direct iconic motivation.

handshape: Open-B both hands flat palms facing up
movement: Single opening movement — directly depicts open book
non_manual_obligatory: No — no obligatory NMM documented
annotator_notes: Highly iconic — hand movement directly depicts the referent object opening. One of clearest classifier-like depictive signs in dataset.

handshape: V-hand — index and middle fingers extended
movement: Alternating finger movement depicting leg motion
non_manual_obligatory: No — no obligatory NMM documented
annotator_notes: Classifier-like depictive sign — V-hand represents legs walking. High iconicity. Common across sign languages with varying handshapes.

handshape: Open-5 or bent-B — fingers spread or loosely bent
movement: Repeated small lateral movement
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — raised eyebrows grammatically obligatory for WH-questions
annotator_notes: Shares grammatical NMM (raised eyebrows) with WHERE — different manual features but same grammatical class. This cross-sign NMM grouping is demonstrated in SPARQL Q3 of the companion RDF dataset.

handshape: Index and middle fingers extended — scissors shape
movement: Lateral wagging then snap closed
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — head shake is grammatical negation marker
annotator_notes: Head shake is grammatically obligatory negation marker — contrastive with YES which uses head nod. Manual + non-manual features both carry grammatical information independently.

handshape: S-hand — closed fist
movement: Nodding / bobbing movement
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — head nod is grammatically obligatory
annotator_notes: Head nod contrastive with head shake on NO. Interesting case where head movement mirrors manual movement — both nod/bob. Question of whether manual movement is motivated by or independent of head NMM.

handshape: G-hand — initialized sign index finger and thumb extended
movement: Single arc path movement
non_manual_obligatory: No — no obligatory NMM documented
annotator_notes: Initialized sign — handshape derived from first letter of English gloss 'G'. Low iconicity. Common in GSL for country and proper noun signs.

handshape: Flat-O / bunched hand — fingertips touching thumb
movement: Fingertips rub against palm repeatedly
non_manual_obligatory: No — no obligatory NMM documented
annotator_notes: High iconicity — rubbing gesture directly depicts handling money. Gestural origin — similar gesture used in many hearing cultures. Cross-cultural iconic motivation.

handshape: Open-B / flat hand — all fingers extended together
movement: Single outward path arc from chin toward interlocutor
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — smile/gratitude expression semantically obligatory
annotator_notes: Social/pragmatic sign. Facial expression (smile) is semantically obligatory — signing THANK YOU with neutral face would be pragmatically marked. Outward path movement iconically depicts giving thanks toward recipient.

handshape: Flat-O hand — variant with fingers more spread
movement: Repeated path toward mouth — faster tempo variant
non_manual_obligatory: Yes — mouth pattern obligatory across variants
annotator_notes: Documented to explore intra-sign variation — same gloss may show handshape and NMM variation across signers or contexts. Faster tempo associated with more animated facial expression.

handshape: W-hand citation form — three fingers cleanly extended thumb tucked
movement: Taps chin twice — cleaner repeated movement than observed production
non_manual_obligatory: Unclear — mouth pattern varies between citation and natural production
annotator_notes: Citation form compared against natural production observed in GSL_002. Handshape difference between citation W-hand and observed loose-finger production illustrates phonetic variation in natural signing. Annotation source: ASL-influenced citation form documentation cross-referenced with Zenodo video.
